//: Copy to guard.config.json at your repo ROOT, then strip these //-comment keys (real JSON has no comments). Every path below is resolved relative to THIS repo (the consumer cwd), never the devkit package dir. Omit any field to keep its built-in default (see gate-engine/config.mjs DEFAULTS).
//boundaries: Cross-trust-boundary path prefixes. The decisions smell gate flags a single change that straddles 2+ of these (a cross-boundary move = likely an architectural decision). Default: [] (no boundaries → smell never fires).
//scanRoots: Where the ratchets + structure scans look for implementation files. Default: ['src'].
//sourceExtensions: Impl-file extensions the fan-out + size ratchets count. Default: ['ts','tsx']. A JS/MJS codebase (a node CLI like devkit itself) sets ['mjs','js'] so the gates actually SEE its files. Tests (*.test.<ext> / *.spec.<ext>) are auto-excluded from impl counts.
//decisionsDir: Append-only decision-log dir (guard-decisions CLI + smell gate target). Default: 'docs/decisions'.
//fanoutCap: Max non-test impl files per folder (any depth) before guard-fanout trips. Default: 12.
//fanoutExempt: Flat-by-design folders exempt from the fanout cap (e.g. one-file-per-domain routers, vendored UI primitives). Default: [].
//thresholds: Matcher/clone tier + size knobs. Override any subset; unspecified knobs keep their default.
//indexPath: search-code embedding index for the semantic duplication matcher. null (or omit) => matcher opts out / fails open; guard-clone + ratchets still run.
//searchTool: Semantic-search tool NAME the search-steering hooks point agents at. Default: 'mcp__codebase__searchCode'. Env override: GUARD_SEARCH_TOOL.
//graphTool: Graph/dependency tool NAME the search-steering hooks point agents at. Default: 'graphify'. Env override: GUARD_GRAPH_TOOL.
//testCommand: Test command the testing agents run (e.g. 'bun run test:run'). Default: null => agents fall back to the package.json test script. Env override: GUARD_TEST_COMMAND.
//review: Topology the 5 reviewer subagents read. backendRoots/frontendRoots = path prefixes per reviewer (frontend reviewers exit early when frontendRoots is empty). trustBoundaries = optional prose describing your client/server split. shortcutTracking = enable the tracked-story rule. accessibility.skipTouchTargets = opt out of the 44px WCAG touch-target check (e.g. a desktop-only app).

review.trustBoundaries: src/main = untrusted client; socket-server + vercel-serverless = trusted backend

//vision: Product-vision judge gate (guard-vision, pre-commit; opt-in via --guards …,vision). You own the CONTENT: statement = prose defining your product's vision and the lines a diff must not cross (what OUT/DRIFT mean for YOUR product). devkit owns the mechanism: FIT/DRIFT/OUT scaffold, opus judge, hard-by-default block on a confident OUT (DRIFT only warns; GUARD_VISION_HARD=0 softens a one-off; GUARD_NO_VISION=1 skips). Omit or null => the gate self-skips.

vision.statement: A friendly AI development TOOL, not a platform: the product must never host the user's shipped-product backend (their database, payments, auth, hosting/deploy). The product's OWN infra is always fine, and the tool ACTING on the user's own external accounts (running their migrations, issuing a refund with their Stripe key, deploying to their own Vercel) as a development task is fine — hosting means WE operate it. OUT = a diff that makes us host, provision or operate the user's product backend. DRIFT = a diff serving an audience off the developer-task spine.
